name: Zoom / Teams for Virtual Town Halls
description: Video conferencing platforms adapted for political town halls, community consultations, and electorate meetings. Virtual events expanded dramatically during COVID and remain a core engagement channel.
whyUseIt: Virtual town halls let politicians engage with constituents who cannot attend in-person events — shift workers, parents with young children, people with disabilities, and those in remote areas. They expand democratic participation beyond the usual community meeting attendees.
aiCapabilities: AI meeting summaries, automated transcription, Q&A management, real-time polls
funnelPosition: Mid funnel — engagement and relationship building
marketerWorkflow: Schedule event, promote via email and social media, set up registration with RSVP tracking, run the event with moderated Q&A, follow up with recording and summary to all registrants
managerExpects: Event report: registrations, actual attendance, questions asked, issues raised, follow-up actions committed

vocabulary.definition: An open community meeting where constituents can ask questions directly to their representative
Questions submitted via chat and selected by a moderator to maintain focus and manage time

beginnerMistakes: Not moderating Q&A — unmoderated chat quickly becomes unproductive
Treating virtual events as one-way broadcasts instead of genuine two-way engagement
